description: Every LemmeBuyIt retail endpoint, plus a local store that compares one UPC across 70+ retailers, ranks weekly price drops, and computes Amazon FBA arbitrage margin — compound queries the per-retailer API can't answer.

tools.name: amazon_get-price-history
tools.description: Returns chart-ready weekly Amazon-side time-series data for a specific ASIN — direct access to amazon_price_history without going through a retailer's product_id. Useful when the consumer already knows the ASIN (e.g., charting the historical-backfill data without a Walmart UPC mapping in the path). The returned series carries Amazon retail / 3P-new / Buy Box prices plus monthly_sold per week, sorted ascending by `ts`. Weeks with no observed samples are omitted. For Walmart-side or merged history, use `/v1/retailers/walmart/products/{product_id}/price-history` instead. Required: asin. Optional: weeks (default: 52).

params.name: asin
params.description: Amazon Standard Identification Number (e.g., 'B0EXAMPLE1').

params.name: weeks
params.description: Number of weeks of history to return (1..104). Defaults to 52.

tools.name: retailers_list
tools.description: Retrieves a list of retailers that the authenticated API key has access to. **Currently Supported Retailers (73 total):** This rollout is configured in `configs/retailers.yaml` and may vary by API key permissions. Use this endpoint to fetch the current retailer list available to the authenticated key. Returns array of Retailer.

tools.name: retailers_categories_get-retailer
tools.description: Retrieves a list of distinct product categories for a specific retailer. Categories are hierarchical with '>' separators and are retailer-specific. ## Common Use Cases **Category Discovery:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/categories # Get all Walmart categories for filtering GET /retailers/homedepot/categories # Get all Home Depot categories for navigation ``` **Integration Patterns:** - Product filtering: Use categories as filter options - Navigation menus: Build hierarchical category trees - Analytics: Category-based reporting - Search refinement: Narrow search results by category **Category Structure Examples:** - 'Clothing>Fashion Brands>Nike>Nike Mens>Nike Mens Clothing' - 'Electronics>Computers & Tablets>Laptops>Gaming Laptops' - 'Home & Garden>Kitchen & Dining>Small Kitchen Appliances' **Performance:** Categories are cached and typically return in under 25ms. Required: retailer_id. Returns array of string.

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: The ID of the retailer. Use the `/retailers` endpoint for the full current list. Examples:...

tools.name: retailers_products_find-retailer-cursor
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of products for a specific retailer with advanced search and filtering capabilities. The API searches across 115+ million products with sub-second response times. ## Search Capabilities ## Common Query Patterns **Text Search with Auto-Detection (NEW!):** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=Nike%20Socks # Auto-detects as text search - Returns 13.6M+ Nike sock products GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=gaming%20laptop&min_price=500&max_price=2000 # Auto-detects as text search - Gaming laptops in $500-2000 price range GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=08004608232285 # Auto-detects as GTIN barcode - Returns specific product GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=123456789012,987654321098 # Auto-detects as multiple GTINs - Returns products matching any barcode GET /retailers/walmart/products?lookup_type=text&search_query=12345 # Force text search even for numeric queries (optional override) ``` **Barcode/Identifier Lookups:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?upc=123456789012 # Filter by UPC barcode (exact match) GET /retailers/walmart/products?ean=1234567890123 # Filter by EAN barcode (exact match) GET /retailers/walmart/products?gtin=12345678901234&condition=New # GTIN filter with condition GET /retailers/walmart/products?mpn=WIDGET-PRO-123&brand=Acme # Filter by Manufacturer Part Number with brand ``` **SKU and Model Searches:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products/873973300 # Get specific product by SKU (direct lookup endpoint) GET /retailers/walmart/products?unique_merchant_sku=873973300 # Search by SKU using filter parameter GET /retailers/walmart/products?model=iPhone-15-Pro&brand=Apple&color=Natural%20Titanium # Find products by model number with attributes ``` **Category & Subcategory Filtering:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?category=Clothing>Fashion%20Brands>Nike # All Nike fashion products GET /retailers/walmart/products?subcategory=Athletic%20Socks&color=Black&size=Large # Black athletic socks in large size GET /retailers/walmart/products?category=Electronics&subcategory=Gaming%20Laptops&min_price=1000 # Gaming laptops over $1000 in Electronics category ``` **Discount & Sale Filtering (Enhanced!):** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?on_sale=true&sort_by=discount_desc # All sale items sorted by highest discount percentage first GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_discount_percent=30 # Products with at least 30% off GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_discount_percent=20&max_discount_percent=50 # Products with 20-50% discount range GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_discount_percent=25&category=Electronics&in_stock=true # Electronics with 25%+ discount that are in stock GET /retailers/walmart/products?on_sale=true&min_discount_percent=40&sort_by=price_asc # Sale items with 40%+ off, sorted by lowest price GET /retailers/walmart/products?brand=Nike&min_discount_percent=15&max_price=100 # Nike products with 15%+ discount under $100 ``` **Advanced Multi-Parameter Filtering:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_price=10&max_price=50&on_sale=true&in_stock=true&currency=USD # Sale items in stock between $10-50 in USD GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_rating=4.5&min_reviews=100&max_shipping=0 # Highly rated products with free shipping GET /retailers/walmart/products?material=Cotton&size=Large&color=Blue&condition=New&in_stock=true # Specific product attributes with stock availability GET /retailers/walmart/products?created_since=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&min_rating=4.0&min_reviews=50&sort_by=created_at_desc # Recently added well-reviewed products GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_weight=1.0&max_weight=5.0&category=Sports%20%26%20Outdoors # Sports equipment within specific weight range GET /retailers/walmart/products?stock_availability=In%20Stock&max_shipping=15.99&min_reviews=10 # In-stock items with affordable shipping and reviews ``` **Sorting Options with Examples:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?category=Electronics&sort_by=price_asc # Electronics sorted by lowest price first GET /retailers/walmart/products?on_sale=true&sort_by=discount_desc # Sale items sorted by highest discount percentage first GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=laptop&sort_by=price_desc # Laptops sorted by highest price first GET /retailers/walmart/products?brand=Nike&sort_by=discount_asc # Nike products sorted by lowest discount first GET /retailers/walmart/products?category=Clothing&sort_by=updated_at_desc # Recently updated clothing items first GET /retailers/walmart/products?min_rating=4.0&sort_by=created_at_desc # Newest highly-rated products first GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=shoes # Shoes with relevance-based sorting (default when sort_by omitted) ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products?search_query=laptop&sort_by=price_desc # Laptops sorted by highest price (premium products first) ``` ## Pagination Guide **Efficient Cursor-Based Pagination:** ``` # Step 1: First request (omit 'after' parameter) GET /retailers/walmart/products?lookup_type=text&search_query=laptop&limit=100&sort_by=price_asc # Response includes next_cursor: 'WzU5OS45OSw3ODkxMjNd' # Step 2: Use cursor for next page GET /retailers/walmart/products?lookup_type=text&search_query=laptop&limit=100&sort_by=price_asc&after=WzU5OS45OSw3ODkxMjNd # Step 3: Continue until next_cursor is null (last page) ``` **Pagination Best Practices:** - **Use consistent parameters**: Keep `search_query`, `limit`, and `sort_by` the same across requests - **Handle large datasets**: Maximum 10,000 items per request - paginate for larger datasets - **Performance optimization**: Smaller page sizes (25-100) provide faster response times - **Error handling**: Check for 400 errors if limit exceeds 10,000 - **Cursor format**: Base64-encoded sort values ensure stable pagination even with data changes **Common Pagination Patterns:** - **Small previews**: `limit=10` for quick product previews - **Standard browsing**: `limit=25-50` for user-facing product lists - **Bulk processing**: `limit=1000-10000` for data analysis or batch operations - **Real-time sync**: Use `sort_by=updated_at_desc` with pagination for incremental updates. Required: retailer_id. Optional: lookup_type, search_query, category (plus 35 more).

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: The ID of the retailer. Use the `/retailers` endpoint for the full current list. Examples:...

params.name: lookup_type
params.description: **OPTIONAL**: Type of lookup to perform when search_query is provided. **Auto-Detection (Recommended)**: When omitted, the API automatically detects the query type: - **Numeric queries** (8-14 digits): Automatically detected as GTIN search - **Text queries**: Automatically detected as text search - **Multiple GTINs**: Comma-separated numeric codes are detected as GTIN search **Manual Override**: You can explicitly specify the lookup type: - `gtin`: Force GTIN/UPC/EAN search mode for identifier lookups - `text`: Force text search mode for name/description matching **Examples of Auto-Detection**: - `search_query=123456789012` → Auto-detected as `gtin` - `search_query=Nike Shoes` → Auto-detected as `text` - `search_query=123456789012,987654321098` → Auto-detected as `gtin` (multiple)

params.name: search_query
params.description: Search query string with automatic detection of search type. **GTIN/Barcode Queries** (automatically detected): - Single GTIN: `123456789012` (8-14 digits) - Multiple GTINs: `123456789012,987654321098` (comma-separated) - Max identifiers per request: 10,000 (configurable) - Searches exact matches in UPC, EAN, GTIN, and MPN fields **Text Queries** (automatically detected): - Product searches: `Nike shoes`, `gaming laptop`, `coffee maker` - Brand queries: `Apple`, `Samsung`, `Nike` - Category searches: `wireless headphones`, `kitchen appliances` - Uses fuzzy matching across names, descriptions, brands, and categories **Auto-Detection Examples:** - Brand + Product: `Nike shoes` → Text search across 13.6M+ results - GTIN lookup: `123456789012` → Exact barcode match - Category search: `gaming laptops` → Text search with fuzzy matching - Multiple codes: `123456789012,987654321098` → Batch GTIN lookup

params.name: category
params.description: Filter by product category (exact match). Categories are hierarchical with '>' separators. **Common Categories:** - 'Clothing>Fashion Brands>Nike>Nike Mens>Nike Mens Clothing>Nike Mens Socks' - 'Electronics>Computers & Tablets>Laptops' - 'Home & Garden>Kitchen & Dining>Small Kitchen Appliances' - 'Sports & Outdoors>Exercise & Fitness>Cardio Equipment'

params.name: min_price
params.description: Filter by minimum product price. Useful for finding premium products or setting budget constraints.

params.name: max_price
params.description: Filter by maximum product price. Combine with min_price for price range filtering.

params.name: brand
params.description: Filter by product brand (exact match). Use exact brand names as they appear in the data. **Popular Brands Available:** - Nike, Adidas, Under Armour (Sportswear) - Apple, Samsung, Sony (Electronics) - KitchenAid, Cuisinart, Hamilton Beach (Kitchen) - Levi's, Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger (Fashion)

params.name: manufacturer
params.description: Filter by product manufacturer (exact match). The manufacturer may be different from the brand in some cases. **Common Manufacturers:** - Oatey (hardware and plumbing supplies) - Nike, Inc. (athletic apparel and footwear) - Apple Inc. (consumer electronics) - Whirlpool Corporation (home appliances)

params.name: on_sale
params.description: Filter for products that are currently on sale (current_price < original_price).

params.name: min_discount_percent
params.description: Filter by minimum discount percentage (0-100). Returns products with discount percentage greater than or equal to this value. Discount is calculated as: ((original_price - current_price) / original_price) * 100

params.name: max_discount_percent
params.description: Filter by maximum discount percentage (0-100). Returns products with discount percentage less than or equal to this value. Useful for finding products within specific discount ranges.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of products to return per page. Defaults to 25, max 10000. Returns 400 error if limit exceeds 10000.

params.name: after
params.description: **Cursor-based pagination parameter** for fetching the next page of results. ## How to Use: 1. **First Request**: Omit this parameter to get the first page 2. **Subsequent Requests**: Use the `next_cursor` value from the previous response 3. **Last Page**: When `next_cursor` is `null`, you've reached the end ## Cursor Format: - Base64-encoded string containing sort values for efficient pagination - Uses Elasticsearch `search_after` for consistent results - Format: `[sort_value_1, sort_value_2, ...]` (price, id, etc.) ## Example Pagination Flow: ``` # Step 1: First page GET /retailers/walmart/products?limit=10&sort_by=price_asc # Returns: next_cursor = 'WzE5Ljk5LDEyMzQ1Nl0=' # Step 2: Second page GET /retailers/walmart/products?limit=10&sort_by=price_asc&after=WzE5Ljk5LDEyMzQ1Nl0= # Returns: next_cursor = 'WzI5Ljk5LDc4OTEyM10=' # Step 3: Continue until next_cursor is null ```

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Sorting order for the results. When not specified, results are returned in the database's physical order for optimal performance. When specified, must be one of the allowed values: - `price_asc`: Sort by price, lowest first - `price_desc`: Sort by price, highest first - `discount_asc`: Sort by discount percentage, lowest first - `discount_desc`: Sort by discount percentage, highest first (best deals first) - `top_seller_rank_asc`: Sort by Amazon sales rank, best rank first - `updated_at_asc/desc`: Sort by last update time - `created_at_asc/desc`: Sort by creation time Note that using explicit sorting may impact query performance on large datasets.

params.name: subcategory
params.description: Filter by product subcategory (exact match).

params.name: currency
params.description: Filter by currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).

params.name: stock_availability
params.description: Filter by stock availability status.

params.name: in_stock
params.description: Filter for products that are currently in stock (boolean).

params.name: upc
params.description: Filter by UPC barcode (exact match). Comma-separated values supported; max 10,000 (configurable).

params.name: ean
params.description: Filter by EAN barcode (exact match). Comma-separated values supported; max 10,000 (configurable).

params.name: gtin
params.description: Filter by GTIN code (exact match). Comma-separated values supported; max 10,000 (configurable).

params.name: asin
params.description: Filter by Amazon ASIN (exact match). Comma-separated values supported; max 10,000 (configurable).

params.name: mpn
params.description: Filter by Manufacturer Part Number (exact match).

params.name: unique_merchant_sku
params.description: Filter by unique merchant SKU (exact match). Comma-separated values supported; max 10,000 (configurable).

params.name: model
params.description: Filter by product model (exact match).

params.name: condition
params.description: Filter by product condition (e.g., New, Used, Refurbished).

params.name: color
params.description: Filter by product color (exact match).

params.name: size
params.description: Filter by product size (exact match).

params.name: material
params.description: Filter by product material (exact match).

params.name: min_weight
params.description: Filter by minimum weight (in product's weight unit).

params.name: max_weight
params.description: Filter by maximum weight (in product's weight unit).

params.name: min_rating
params.description: Filter by minimum product rating (0-5 scale).

params.name: max_rating
params.description: Filter by maximum product rating (0-5 scale).

params.name: min_reviews
params.description: Filter by minimum number of reviews.

params.name: min_sales_rank
params.description: Filter by minimum Amazon sales rank.

params.name: max_sales_rank
params.description: Filter by maximum Amazon sales rank.

params.name: max_shipping
params.description: Filter by maximum shipping cost.

params.name: created_since
params.description: Filter products created since this date (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: updated_since
params.description: Filter products updated since this date (ISO 8601 format).

tools.name: retailers_products_get-price-history
tools.description: Returns chart-ready weekly time-series data for a product, sourced from the daily price-history sampler + the one-time historical backfill. When the product's UPC maps to a resolved Amazon ASIN, Amazon retail / 3P-new / Buy-Box / monthly-sold series are merged on weekly timestamps alongside Walmart's own price series. Weeks with no observed samples are omitted. Use the rolled-up averages on the `Product.price_averages` block when you just need the lifetime / 30 / 90 / 180 / 365-day means. Use this endpoint when you actually want to plot a chart. Required: retailer_id, product_id. Optional: weeks (default: 52).

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: Retailer ID. Use the `/retailers` endpoint for the full list.

params.name: product_id
params.description: Retailer-native product identifier (Walmart product_id, Home Depot SKU, etc.).

params.name: weeks
params.description: Number of weeks of history to return (1..104). Defaults to 52.

tools.name: retailers_products_get-retailer-details-by-sku
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific product within a retailer's catalog, looking up by Unique Merchant SKU. ## Common Use Cases **Direct Product Access:** ``` GET /retailers/walmart/products/226595547 # Get Nike sock product by SKU GET /retailers/walmart/products/ASUS-ROG-G15-2024 # Get gaming laptop by alphanumeric SKU GET /retailers/homedepot/products/HD-12345-XL # Get Home Depot product by SKU format ``` **Integration Patterns:** - Product detail pages: Use SKU from search results - Inventory management: Direct SKU lookups - Price monitoring: Regular SKU-based checks - Catalog synchronization: Bulk SKU validation **Performance:** Sub-50ms response times for direct SKU lookups with full product details. Required: retailer_id, product_id. Returns the Product.

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: The ID of the retailer. Use the `/retailers` endpoint for the full current list. Examples:...

params.name: product_id
params.description: The Unique Merchant SKU of the product. SKU formats vary by retailer: - Walmart: Numeric (e.g., '226595547') or alphanumeric (e.g., 'ASUS-ROG-G15-2024') - Home Depot: Typically alphanumeric with retailer prefix (e.g., 'HD-12345-XL')

tools.name: retailers_shopping_get-retailer-product-details-by-sku
tools.description: Retrieves a shopping product within a retailer's catalog by Unique Merchant SKU. The response uses the shopping-safe response contract and includes the shopping destination URL as `product_url`. Required: retailer_id, product_id. Returns array of string.

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: The ID of the retailer.

params.name: product_id
params.description: The Unique Merchant SKU of the product.

tools.name: retailers_shopping_get-retailer-products
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of shopping products for a specific retailer. Shopping products use a shopping-safe response contract and only include products with a shopping destination URL, returned as `product_url`. This endpoint supports the same API-key authentication, rate limiting, usage tracking, and retailer authorization as retailer product endpoints. Unsupported shopping query parameters return `400 Bad Request`: - `asin` - `min_sales_rank` - `max_sales_rank` - `sort_by=top_seller_rank_asc`. Required: retailer_id. Optional: search_query, lookup_type, unique_merchant_sku (plus 12 more). Returns array of ShoppingProduct.

params.name: retailer_id
params.description: The ID of the retailer.

params.name: search_query
params.description: Text, SKU, UPC, EAN, GTIN, or MPN search query.

params.name: lookup_type
params.description: Optional lookup mode override. Omit `lookup_type` for auto-detection.

params.name: unique_merchant_sku
params.description: Filter by retailer SKU.

params.name: upc
params.description: Filter by UPC barcode.

params.name: ean
params.description: Filter by EAN barcode.

params.name: gtin
params.description: Filter by GTIN barcode.

params.name: mpn
params.description: Filter by Manufacturer Part Number.

params.name: min_price
params.description: Minimum current price.

params.name: max_price
params.description: Maximum current price.

params.name: brand
params.description: Filter by brand.

params.name: category
params.description: Filter by category.

params.name: in_stock
params.description: Filter by stock status.

params.name: sort_by
params.description: Sort order. `top_seller_rank_asc` is not supported by shopping endpoints.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of products to return.

params.name: after
params.description: Cursor for the next page of results.

tools.name: status_get
tools.description: Provides the current operational status of the API service, including database connectivity. Returns the StatusResponse.
